mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate compresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates and reduced corporate borrowing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A spike in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a deterioration in labor demand and consumer spending, which erodes corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE exceeds 5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which boosts revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between joint increase signal and 20-day forward returns is below 0.1 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2% year-over-year, the economy is in a self-reinforcing expansion that lifts earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, which typically precedes positive equity returns as economic activity accelerates.
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% signals an aggressively tight monetary stance that directly increases the cost of capital and reduces equity risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS > 4.0 is not negative or fails a one-sided t-test (p>0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low unemployment combined with very high payrolls indicates an overheated labor market that forces wage inflation and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) when UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million annualized and retail sales above $730,000 million jointly signal sustained housing-led demand, which provides a positive impulse to the S&P 500 over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return in triggered periods is not positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over any 3-year window.
